2605.10373.title: Toward Multi-Database Query Reasoning for Text2Cypher
2605.10373.paper_summary: Large language models have significantly improved natural language interfaces to databases by translating user questions into executable queries. In particular, Text2Cypher focuses on generating Cypher queries for graph databases, enabling users to access graph data without query language expertise. Most existing Text2Cypher systems assume a single preselected graph database, where queries are generated over a known schema. However, real-world systems are often distributed across multiple independent graph databases organized by domain or system boundaries, where relevant information may span multiple sources. To address this limitation, we propose a shift from single-database query generation to multi-database query reasoning. Instead of assuming a fixed execution context, the system must reason about (i) relevant databases, (ii) how to decompose a question across them, and (iii) how to integrate partial results. We formalize this setting through a three-phase roadmap: database routing, multi-database decomposition, and heterogeneous query reasoning across database types and query languages. This work provides a structured formulation of multi-database reasoning for Text2Cypher and identifies challenges in source selection, query decomposition, and result integration, aiming to support more realistic and scalable natural language interfaces to graph databases.
2605.10373.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型顯著改善了自然語言與資料庫之間的介面，通過將用戶問題轉換為可執行的查詢。<lb/>特別是，Text2Cypher 專注於為圖形資料庫生成 Cypher 查詢，使得用戶能夠在不具備查詢語言專業知識的情況下訪問圖形數據。<lb/>大多數現有的 Text2Cypher 系統假設一個單一的預選圖形資料庫，查詢是在已知的模式上生成的。<lb/>然而，現實世界的系統通常分佈在多個獨立的圖形資料庫中，這些資料庫按領域或系統邊界組織，相關信息可能跨越多個來源。<lb/>為了解決這一限制，我們提出從單一資料庫查詢生成轉向多資料庫查詢推理。<lb/>系統必須推理 (i) 相關的資料庫，(ii) 如何在它們之間分解問題，以及 (iii) 如何整合部分結果，而不是假設固定的執行上下文。<lb/>我們通過三階段的路線圖來形式化這一設定：資料庫路由、多資料庫分解，以及跨資料庫類型和查詢語言的異質查詢推理。<lb/>這項工作為 Text2Cypher 提供了多資料庫推理的結構化表述，並確定了在來源選擇、查詢分解和結果整合方面的挑戰，旨在支持更現實和可擴展的自然語言介面來訪問圖形資料庫。
2605.10373.author: Makbule Gulcin Ozsoy et.al.
